title: Synchronize committed evidence with published v0.5.28
outOfScope: Rewriting or moving the immutable v0.5.28 tag or GitHub Release
Changing runtime behavior or release workflow policy

sources.reason: Defines the tag-aware computation that smoke uses to validate committed evidence.
Committed SBOM was still bound to the previous public tag when smoke ran after v0.5.28 publication.
Must match the published v0.5.28 source identity and regenerated SBOM digest.
Must match the synchronized published evidence artifacts.
Canonical release metadata stores the synchronized evidence digests.
Defines the bounded post-publication evidence synchronization.
Existing baseline test is the direct regression check for committed evidence consistency.

riskAssessment.reason: Committed evidence must match the immutable published tag so post-release smoke can verify repository truth.

executionDecision.reason: The post-release smoke failure provides concrete evidence of stale committed evidence; the immutable tag remains unchanged.

preReviewWarnings: Verify all regenerated artifacts resolve v0.5.28 and no historical tag or release object is changed.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record evidence synchronization checkpoints before and after regeneration.

acceptance: Committed SBOM, provenance, and release-digests resolve to the immutable v0.5.28 source tag.
release.json digests match the synchronized committed evidence and all local quality gates pass.
Strict post-release smoke passes without changing the v0.5.28 tag or GitHub Release.
guidelines: Use the existing tag-aware supply-chain generator; do not hand-edit generated evidence.
Keep the immutable v0.5.28 tag and GitHub Release unchanged.
Regenerate all dependent digests together and verify their consistency.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: committed evidence resolves v0.5.28 rather than v0.5.27
immutable tag and release remain unchanged

intent.problem: Post-release smoke found that committed SBOM evidence was generated while v0.5.28 was pending and therefore still used the previous public tag; the published release is correct but repository evidence is stale.
constraints: Use the existing generator with the published tag present.
Keep release.json and dependent digests internally consistent.
intent.rationale: Regenerating the committed evidence after publication aligns the repository baseline with the immutable release identity and allows the existing smoke verification to pass.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User explicitly required the remediation loop to continue through complete release verification.

rollbackNote: Revert only the post-release evidence synchronization; do not rewrite the immutable v0.5.28 tag or release.
